docs.body:
# Week 6.5d — PR notes (scratch, lifted into PR body at push time)

## Scope

- Ships `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` (sandbox-side binary wrapping `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk`'s `query()`) + `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` (host-side HTTP translator from the Anthropic Messages API → `llm:call`).
- Adds a `runner: z.enum(['native', 'claude-sdk']).default('native')` discriminator to `@ax/cli`'s config; selecting `claude-sdk` swaps the sandbox runner binary AND registers the proxy plugin.
- Extends `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` with `llm-proxy:start` / `llm-proxy:stop` lifecycle calls + an `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` env injection into the runner child.
- Adds a `test-host-echo` stub plugin to `@ax/test-harness` used by the new acceptance test to exercise the MCP host-tool path.
- No new IPC actions (per plan I2). `tool.execute-host` (schema-only in 6.5a) earns its runtime wiring here.
- Host-side chat behavior with `runner: 'native'` is unchanged; `@ax/llm-anthropic`, `@ax/audit-log`, `@ax/storage-sqlite`, `@ax/chat-orchestrator`, `@ax/ipc-server` untouched except for lockfile bumps.

## Branch note

Branched from `main` (tip of 6.5a, `f07a6cf`). The design doc's kickoff prompt said "branch off 6.5c," but 6.5b and 6.5c were skipped for MVP per the project MVP direction memo (2026-04-24).

## Commits

Grouped by concern; full chain is `git log --oneline main..HEAD` (20 commits).

**Scaffolding**

- `6a2ee84` scaffold(6.5d): `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format` + `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` skeletons
- `752bbf9` chore(6.5d): drop agent-claude-sdk-runner barrel; point exports at main.js

**Proxy (`@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format`)**

- `fafaf2a` feat(llm-proxy): anthropic ↔ LlmRequest translation (pure functions)
- `7163b66` fix(llm-proxy): code-review follow-ups — reserved prefixes, role-appropriate blocks, nits
- `fa2225b` feat(llm-proxy): synthesized anthropic SSE frames
- `46bbac1` feat(llm-proxy): HTTP listener + plugin + sandbox-subprocess AX_LLM_PROXY_URL wiring
- `9375c39` fix(llm-proxy): code-review follow-ups — lint, tighter error messages, trimmed banners

**Runner (`@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`)**

- `f99e74a` feat(claude-sdk-runner): env validation + AX_LLM_PROXY_URL plumbing
- `4d75f7a` feat(claude-sdk-runner): tool-name classifier
- `cbeef79` feat(claude-sdk-runner): canUseTool → tool.pre-call adapter
- `deee4ec` feat(claude-sdk-runner): PostToolUse → event.tool-post-call adapter
- `26e4589` feat(claude-sdk-runner): host MCP server for `executesIn: 'host'` tools
- `c578a13` feat(claude-sdk-runner): main entry wiring `query()` + canUseTool + hooks + MCP
- `0d0b6ad` fix(claude-sdk-runner): swallow `client.close()` errors at chat exit
- `d474ce9` test(claude-sdk-runner): unit-level `main()` flow with mocked SDK + IPC

**CLI + harness + acceptance**

- `167b982` feat(cli): runner config discriminator for native vs claude-sdk
- `9eb3471` feat(test-harness): test-host-echo stub for host-mediated tool tests
- `e54840e` test(cli): acceptance — claude-sdk built-in + MCP host tools fire subscribers

**Docs**

- `ac43bae` docs(6.5d): SECURITY.md for llm-proxy + claude-sdk-runner (security-checklist output)
- `3392638` chore(6.5d): exact-pin claude-agent-sdk + test `settingSources` contract

## Invariants (I1–I9)

Carry-forwards from 6.5a's deviations, enumerated as numbered invariants in the plan so the reviewer can audit against them.

- **I1 — runner holds no LLM credentials.** The sandbox never sees a real `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`. The env var passed to claude-sdk IS the per-session bearer token (same token that authenticates IPC). The proxy validates it via `session:resolve-token`. Enforced by `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts` (sets `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: env.authToken`) + `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/listener.ts` (bearer check before body read).
- **I2 — schema source of truth stays in `@ax/ipc-protocol`.** No new IPC actions in 6.5d. `tool.execute-host` (schema-only in 6.5a) earns runtime wiring here; `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/host-mcp-server.ts` forwards to it.
- **I3 — proxy HTTP surface is a new attack surface.** Bound to `127.0.0.1:<ephemeral>`; `Authorization: Bearer` check runs BEFORE any body read; body-size cap = 4 MiB (matches `@ax/ipc-server`); auth token never echoed. Enforced by `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/listener.ts` + covered by 22 listener tests.
- **I4 — no half-wired plugins.** Both new packages are reachable from the acceptance test. The runner binary is resolved in `@ax/cli` via `createRequire` (same mechanism as `@ax/agent-native-runner`); the proxy plugin is registered conditionally when `runner === 'claude-sdk'`.
- **I5 — honest tool name passthrough.** The claude-sdk tool name (`Bash`, `Read`, `mcp__ax-host-tools__<name>`, …) flows verbatim into `tool:pre-call` / `tool:post-call` subscribers. No remap. Enforced by `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/tool-names.ts` (classifier) + asserted by the acceptance test (`preCallEvents.map((e) => e.call.name)` equals `['Bash', 'mcp__ax-host-tools__test-host-echo']`).
- **I6 — no new dependency on sandbox-side impl packages.** claude-sdk ships its own `Bash`/`Read`/`Write`/`Edit`. `@ax/tool-bash-impl` and `@ax/tool-file-io-impl` stay wired to `@ax/agent-native-runner` only. Enforced by `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/package.json` (no such deps).
- **I7 — turn boundary = `SDKResultMessage`.** `event.turn-end` and `event.chat-end` fire on the SDK's terminal `result` message, never mid-stream. Enforced by `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/src/main.ts` (`msg.type === 'result'` branch).
- **I8 — streaming SSE is synthesized, not pass-through.** IPC `llm.call` is non-streaming. The proxy issues one IPC call per `/v1/messages` request and emits faux SSE frames. Enforced by `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/sse-frames.ts` + covered by dedicated tests.
- **I9 — proxy listener lifecycle mirrors IPC listener.** Per-session bind, started alongside `ipc:start` and torn down alongside `ipc:stop`. Proxy-start failure rolls back `ipc:stop` + `session:terminate` + tempdir cleanup (same shape as `ipc:start` rollback). Enforced by `packages/sandbox-subprocess/src/open-session.ts` + covered by rollback test.

## Scope decisions

The 10 locked-in calls from the plan, each with a one-line status.

1. **Streaming vs non-streaming proxy.** Both supported. Non-streaming = direct JSON response; streaming = synthesized SSE frames from one bulk `llm:call`. Token-level re-streaming from the provider is not implemented (that was 6.5b, skipped).
2. **Tool name convention.** claude-sdk names pass verbatim to host subscribers (`Bash`, `mcp__ax-host-tools__<name>`). The runner strips the `mcp__ax-host-tools__` prefix only when dispatching to `tool.execute-host` (host expects bare name).
3. **MCP server grouping.** One `createSdkMcpServer({ name: 'ax-host-tools' })` per runner; all `executesIn: 'host'` tools register into it.
4. **Disabled built-ins.** `disallowedTools: ['WebFetch', 'WebSearch', 'Skill', 'Task']`. Deny-list, not allow-list (fragile when SDK adds built-ins).
5. **`settingSources: []`.** AX owns the context; no CLAUDE.md / project-settings bleed-through. Pinned by test in `3392638`.
6. **System prompt.** `{ type: 'preset', preset: 'claude_code' }`. The runner's job is to be claude-sdk; reinventing the prompt would defeat the point.
7. **`pathToClaudeCodeExecutable`.** Unset. The SDK bundles its own claude-code CLI; pnpm hoisting makes it reachable from the sandbox's `node_modules`.
8. **Proxy auth scheme.** Reuses the existing session bearer token. No new auth boundary. The proxy calls `session:resolve-token`, same path `@ax/ipc-server` uses.
9. **Proxy transport.** Plain HTTP on `127.0.0.1:<ephemeral>` per session, URL injected via `AX_LLM_PROXY_URL` → claude-sdk's `ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL`. k8s / Docker sandboxes will need to re-route (Week 7–9, flagged below).
10. **Built-in vs MCP `tool:pre-call` parity.** Both paths fire `tool:pre-call` with `{id, name, input}` and respect the verdict. `canUseTool` synthesizes a runner-local UUID for the pre-call (no `tool_use_id` available at that stage); `PostToolUse` emits the real `tool_use_id`. We do not expose a pre↔post correlation contract in 6.5d (post is observation-only per D4).

## Security-checklist summary

Both files follow the three-threat-model walk (sandbox escape, prompt injection, supply chain) prescribed by the `security-checklist` skill.

- **`packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/SECURITY.md`** — loopback-only `127.0.0.1` bind; `Authorization: Bearer` check runs before any body parse; 4 MiB body cap (Content-Length fail-fast + mid-stream); auth token never echoed in error responses; per-session ephemeral port; no new runtime dependencies beyond `zod` (already in the workspace). Prompt-injection verdict: the proxy is a translator, not a firewall — scrubbing belongs to `llm:pre-call` subscribers on the host. Supply chain: no new top-level deps.
- **`packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/SECURITY.md`** — `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk` pinned to exact patch `0.2.119`; `WebFetch` / `WebSearch` / `Skill` / `Task` explicitly disabled as a capability minimization (documented so a future reviewer must re-run the checklist before re-enabling any of them); `settingSources: []` prevents external skill / settings bleed-through; lockfile diff spot-checked. Zod peer-dep mismatch (workspace zod@3 vs SDK peer zod@4) documented and accepted for MVP (see "Open issues" below).

## Boundary review

### `llm-proxy:start` / `llm-proxy:stop` (`@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format`)

- **Alternate impl:** `@ax/llm-proxy-mock` (tests) or `@ax/llm-proxy-openai-format` (future provider). The start/stop surface is intentionally format-agnostic.
- **Payload fields:** `sessionId` (canonical), `url` (generic HTTP URL), `port` (numeric). No Anthropic-specific names leak.
- **Subscriber risk:** N/A — service hooks, no subscribers.
- **Wire surface:** the proxy's `POST /v1/messages` IS a wire surface, but it's an external API (Anthropic Messages spec), not an AX IPC action. `@ax/ipc-protocol` schemas unaffected. The Anthropic request/response Zod schemas live in `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/src/anthropic-schemas.ts`.

### `sandbox:open-session` extension (new `calls:` entries)

`@ax/sandbox-subprocess` now declares `calls: ['llm-proxy:start', 'llm-proxy:stop']` on its manifest. The session-lifetime contract is unchanged; the extension is additive (best-effort `stop` on close, rollback on failed `start`). No boundary-review re-review needed per the CLAUDE.md "internal-only changes" carve-out — the hook surface itself is owned by `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format`.

## Acceptance

- **Test file:** `packages/cli/src/__tests__/claude-sdk-runner.e2e.test.ts`
- **Run:** `pnpm --filter @ax/cli test claude-sdk-runner -v`
- **What it asserts:** spawns a real subprocess sandbox running the real `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner` binary (not mocked); registers an observer plugin that subscribes to `tool:pre-call` and `tool:post-call`; registers `createTestHostToolPlugin()` (the `test-host-echo` stub); swaps `llm:call` with a canned 3-response sequence (Bash tool_use → MCP tool_use → terminal text). Asserts both `Bash` and `mcp__ax-host-tools__test-host-echo` fire `tool:pre-call` AND `tool:post-call` in order, and the CLI exits `0`.
- **Runtime:** ~2s locally. CI timeout 60s.

## Open issues / known gaps

Called out explicitly so the reviewer doesn't have to dig.

1. **Branch target is `main`, not 6.5c.** 6.5b (token-level streaming) and 6.5c skipped for MVP per `project_mvp_direction.md`. Plan-file noted this; calling it out here too.
2. **`llm:pre-call` / `llm:post-call` subscribers are NOT wired at runtime.** The plan defers these to "a future slice when the first subscriber (canary) earns its keep." The proxy calls `llm:call` directly; no transformation chain.
3. **Zod peer-dep mismatch.** Workspace uses `zod@^3.23.8`; `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119` declares `zod@^4.0.0` as a peer. At runtime the SDK works against zod@3's surface (we exercise it in the acceptance test); documented in `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/SECURITY.md`. Upgrading the whole workspace to zod@4 is a separate slice.
4. **k8s / Docker sandbox plugins will need to re-route proxy HTTP.** The proxy binds to `127.0.0.1` on the host; subprocess sandbox inherits the host's network namespace, so this works today. k8s pod-per-session (Week 7–9) will have to surface the proxy inside the pod's namespace — either by sidecar or by a cluster-HTTP URL in place of loopback. Not blocking MVP.
5. **Task 13 `test-host-echo` stub relies on the dispatcher passing the full `{id, name, input}` envelope.** Candidate for a documented boundary contract when the first real host-mediated tool plugin lands (likely 6.5e `mcp-client`). Not a bug today — the contract is enforced by the acceptance test — but worth codifying when a second consumer arrives.
6. **e2e test (`claude-sdk-runner.e2e.test.ts`) skips on CI.** The claude-agent-sdk ships precompiled native `claude` binaries (~150 MB each) as platform-specific optional deps. pnpm only installs the variant matching the host's platform/libc, so a lockfile baked on a Mac dev machine doesn't carry the Linux binary CI needs. The test detects binary presence via `createRequire` across all variants and skips with `describe.skipIf(...)` when none resolves. Unit-level coverage (`@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`'s `main.test.ts`) still runs on CI. Future fix: install the right SDK variant in CI via `.npmrc` `supportedArchitectures` or a CI-only step that downloads the binary.

## Out of scope

Re-asserted from the plan, unchanged.

- Token-level streaming from the provider (6.5b-style incremental rendering) — Week 13+.
- Real user-configured MCP servers (stdio / http / sse) — 6.5e.
- Non-Anthropic providers (OpenAI, Gemini) — Week 13+.
- k8s sandbox & multi-replica proxy topology — Week 7–9.
- Image content-block support in the proxy — Week 13+.
- `llm:pre-call` / `llm:post-call` subscriber wiring — future slice, see Open Issue #2.

## Files of note

- `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5d-claude-sdk-runner.md` — the 16-task plan.
- `.changeset/week-6-5d-claude-sdk-runner.md` — per-package version bumps.
- `packages/llm-proxy-anthropic-format/SECURITY.md` — proxy threat-model review.
- `packages/agent-claude-sdk-runner/SECURITY.md` — runner + SDK dep review.
- `packages/cli/src/__tests__/claude-sdk-runner.e2e.test.ts` — acceptance test.

# Week 6.5e — PR notes (scratch, lifted into PR body at push time)

## Summary

Week 6.5e ships two new plugins: `@ax/credentials` (at-rest encrypted secrets, AES-256-GCM via `node:crypto`, key sourced from `AX_CREDENTIALS_KEY`) and `@ax/mcp-client` (host-side Model Context Protocol server hosting — stdio + streamable-http + SSE transports, per-server connection lifecycle with reconnect backoff). The canary is `packages/cli/src/__tests__/mcp-client.e2e.test.ts` — it spawns the real `@ax/mcp-client` against a real stdio MCP subprocess (`mcp-server-stub` from `@ax/test-harness`), calls a tool successfully, crashes the server, and verifies the recovery path surfaces `MCP_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE` without terminating chat. Runs in ~854ms locally.

## Scope

What shipped:

- **`@ax/credentials`** — three service hooks (`credentials:set`, `credentials:get`, `credentials:delete`), AES-256-GCM round-trip through `storage:set` / `storage:get`, 32-byte key read once from `AX_CREDENTIALS_KEY` at plugin init (hex or base64 accepted), ID validation `^[a-z0-9][a-z0-9_.-]{0,127}$`, tombstone delete (encrypted empty string) until `storage:delete` lands. Host-side only — not exposed on the IPC bridge to sandboxes.
- **`@ax/mcp-client`** — per-server `McpConnection` (`disconnected → connecting → ready` with `unhealthy` recoverable branch and `closed` one-way terminus); stdio / streamable-http / SSE transports via `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0` (exact-pinned to match the transitive resolution already pulled in by `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119`); tool-name namespacing (`mcp.<serverId>.<sanitized>`, with duplicate-remote-tool rejection and hash-suffix collision handling); reconnect backoff `1s, 2s, 4s, 8s, 16s` capped at 16s; `MCP_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE` wraps connection failures as tool-error results the model can reason about; plain `http://` URLs log `mcp_plain_http_transport` at startup.
- **`@ax/cli`** — new `ax-next credentials set <id>` subcommand (stdin-only; no argv value to avoid shell history), `ax-next mcp add|list|rm|test` subcommands; both plugins conditionally registered on the chat path based on the user's `ax.config.ts`.
- **`@ax/test-harness`** — `mcp-server-stub` (minimal stdio MCP server exposing `echo` and `crash` tools) for acceptance coverage.

What was deferred (target weeks in parens):

- Admin HTTP API for MCP server configs (Week 9.5 / 10–12) — configs today are edited via CLI subcommands; no admin endpoint yet.
- Per-agent MCP scoping (Week 9.5) — every MCP server is reachable from every agent in the current CLI process until the auth slice lands.
- `ax-next credentials rotate` subcommand (follow-up issue) — today's rotation means restart + re-set each credential.
- `storage:delete` service hook (separate slice) — `credentials:delete` is a tombstone write until then.
- OAuth flows for MCP servers requiring them (Week 13+) — bearer tokens via `headerCredentialRefs` work today.
- Dynamic MCP config reload (Week 13+) — `ax-next mcp add` requires a CLI restart to pick up.
- ulimits / cgroups for stdio MCP subprocesses (Week 13+) — a badly-written community MCP server can OOM the host or spin CPU.
- TLS certificate pinning for HTTP / SSE MCP transports (Week 13+) — trust the system CA bundle today.
- KMS-backed encryption key provider (Week 13+) — `AX_CREDENTIALS_KEY` env-var is the MVP sentinel.
- Prefix-scan in `@ax/storage-sqlite` (separate slice) — `ax-next mcp list` today reads a `mcp:servers` index key because `storage:get` is exact-key only.
- Token-level streaming from the MCP subprocess (Week 13+) — tool results return bulk.

## Boundary review

This slice adds two new hook surfaces: `credentials:{set,get,delete}` and no new MCP-specific hooks (mcp-client consumes the existing `tool:register` / `tool:execute:${name}` / `storage:*` / `credentials:get` surfaces as a producer).

### `credentials:set` / `credentials:get` / `credentials:delete` (`@ax/credentials`)

- **Alternate impl this hook surface could have:** `@ax/credentials-kms` — same three service hooks, but backed by a cloud KMS decrypt call instead of local AES-GCM. The service hook signatures are plaintext-in / plaintext-out to the caller, so swapping the implementation requires no change at the call site.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. The service hooks use `id` (opaque string) and `value` (opaque string). No mention of `key`, `iv`, `tag`, `cipher`, `kms-arn`, `rotation-id`, or any other backend-specific vocabulary on the hook surface. The `credential:` storage-key prefix is an internal implementation detail and does not appear in any hook payload.
- **Subscriber risk:** no subscribers today. A future `@ax/audit-log` subscriber keying off `id` is fine — `id` is vendor-neutral. A subscriber that tried to peek at ciphertext would have to intercept `storage:set`, which is a different boundary.
- **Wire surface:** none. `credentials:*` is host-side only; it is NOT exposed on the IPC bridge to sandboxes. If we ever do expose it, the auth story (per-session scoping, per-credential ACL) has to land first.

### `@ax/mcp-client` — no new hooks, new producer on existing ones

- **Alternate impl:** any other dynamic-tool-source plugin. An OpenAPI-to-tool bridge, an LLM-as-tool plugin, or a Home-Assistant-to-tool adapter would implement the same producer shape — call `tool:register` per discovered tool at init, service `tool:execute:${name}` at call time.
- **Payload fields that might leak:** `transport` can be `'stdio' | 'streamable-http' | 'sse'`, which is closed MCP vocabulary (any MCP-speaking implementation uses the same three names), not backend-specific. `credentialRefs` / `headerCredentialRefs` values are opaque credential IDs, saying nothing about whether the backend is env-var / KMS / Vault.
- **Subscriber risk:** none new. Existing subscribers (dispatcher catalog, observers) already handle `executesIn: 'host'`.
- **Wire surface:** no new IPC actions. Sandboxes invoke MCP tools through the existing `tool.execute-host` IPC handler (schema-only in 6.5a, runtime-wired in 6.5d).

## Security review

Both plugins landed with full `security-checklist` SECURITY.md files. Condensed highlights:

- **`packages/credentials/SECURITY.md`** — No new runtime deps (`node:crypto` + `@ax/core` only). AES-256-GCM is standard AEAD: 32-byte key, 12-byte random IV per encryption, 16-byte tag. Key is read once from env at plugin init, held in a closure for process lifetime; never logged, never returned over a hook. Decrypt failures return scrubbed `PluginError` that never echo ciphertext or key material. Credential IDs are validated before storage, then namespaced under `credential:` to prevent plugin-storage collisions. Known limits: no audit logging of reads (future subscriber), no per-credential ACL (Week 9.5 auth slice), no memory zeroization (V8-level concern, Week 13+ KMS), no ciphertext version byte (migration cost if we swap algorithms).
- **`packages/mcp-client/SECURITY.md`** — One new runtime dep: `@modelcontextprotocol/sdk@1.29.0`, exact-pinned to align with the transitive resolution from `@anthropic-ai/claude-agent-sdk@0.2.119` (a caret range would double our audit surface). No `preinstall` / `postinstall` / `install` / `prepare` hooks in the SDK. Stdio subprocesses spawn with a short env allowlist (`PATH`, `HOME`, `LANG`, `LC_ALL`) plus `config.env` plus resolved `credentialRefs`; we explicitly pass `env` to `StdioClientTransport` so the SDK does NOT fall back to `getDefaultEnvironment()`. Inline-secret keys in the config (`password`, `secret`, `token`, `apikey`, `api_key` at any depth) are rejected at parse time. Argv is `{command, args}` with no shell interpolation. Tool descriptions / results are treated as opaque third-party content — never interpolated into shells, paths, queries, or log lines. Known limits: no subprocess resource caps (Week 13+), no TLS cert pinning (Week 13+), no OAuth flows (Week 13+), no per-agent scoping (Week 9.5).

## Tests

Total repo tests (post-slice): **580** across 23 packages, all green.

New in this slice (~128 tests):

- **`@ax/credentials`** — 16 tests across `crypto.test.ts` (AES-256-GCM round-trip, tampered tag, wrong key, short ciphertext, key parse) and `plugin.test.ts` (service-hook registration, set/get/delete round-trip, invalid ID, empty-string tombstone, missing-key init failure).
- **`@ax/mcp-client`** — 76 tests across `config.test.ts` (schema, inline-secret rejection, credential-ref resolution, cycle guard), `transports.test.ts` (stdio env shaping, streamable-http, sse, plain-http warning), `connection.test.ts` (state machine, connect from unhealthy, disconnect error swallowing, listTools, callTool), `connection-recovery.test.ts` (`MCP_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE`, backoff schedule, reset on reconnect), `tool-names.test.ts` (namespacing, collision hash-suffix, duplicate rejection, length cap), `plugin.test.ts` (init wiring, `tool:register` fan-out, `tool:execute:${name}` dispatch).
- **`@ax/test-harness`** — 4 new tests covering `mcp-server-stub` (protocol handshake, tool listing, echo call, crash exit).
- **`@ax/cli`** — 13 new tests across `credentials-cli.test.ts` (stdin-only input, missing-key error, invalid-ID error, success path, no-argv-value safety), `credentials-wiring.test.ts` (plugin registration on chat path), `mcp-cli.test.ts` (add/list/rm/test subcommands, validation errors, HTTPS warning), and 2 new in `mcp-client.e2e.test.ts` / 2 more split across `e2e.test.ts` + `e2e-real-llm.test.ts` adjustments.

Noteworthy nuances documented for future readers:

- The acceptance test post-call output is double-wrapped via the IPC handler — the outer envelope is the handler's structured response, the inner is MCP's tool-result shape. Future tool-observer plugins unwrapping this should do it explicitly.
- The `crash` tool race: the FIRST `callTool` invocation on a crash-tool also returns `MCP_SERVER_UNAVAILABLE`, because the subprocess exits during (not before) the JSON-RPC response. That's the path we want — the plugin treats both flavors the same way — but it's worth knowing the crash assertion is "you got an error," not "you got a specific flavor of error."

## Follow-ups (file as GitHub issues post-merge)

Candidate issue titles, one per deferral:

1. `@ax/credentials`: add `ax-next credentials rotate` subcommand
2. `@ax/storage-sqlite`: add `storage:delete` service hook (lets `credentials:delete` drop the tombstone)
3. `@ax/storage-sqlite`: add a `storage:list-prefix` hook (lets `@ax/mcp-client` drop the explicit `mcp:servers` index key)
4. `@ax/credentials`: add audit-log subscriber on `credentials:get`
5. `@ax/credentials`: ciphertext version byte + migration story
6. `@ax/credentials`: KMS-backed key provider (env-var replacement)
7. `@ax/mcp-client`: TLS certificate pinning for HTTP / SSE transports
8. `@ax/mcp-client`: OAuth flows for MCP servers that require them
9. `@ax/mcp-client`: dynamic config reload (no CLI restart needed)
10. `@ax/mcp-client`: ulimits / cgroups for stdio MCP subprocesses
11. `@ax/mcp-client` / `@ax/auth`: per-agent scoping (Week 9.5)
12. Admin HTTP API for MCP server configs (Week 9.5 / 10–12)
13. `@ax/mcp-client`: evaluate forking SDK to strip server half (drops transitive `express` / `hono`)

## Architecture / invariant audit

Which of the five invariants this slice exercised:

- **I1 (hook surface transport/storage-agnostic):** `credentials:*` uses `id` / `value`; no mention of `iv`, `tag`, `cipher`, `kms-arn`. `@ax/mcp-client` uses MCP-vocabulary closed names (`stdio | streamable-http | sse`), not backend-specific storage names. Boundary review passed.
- **I2 (no cross-plugin imports):** `@ax/mcp-client` talks to `@ax/credentials` only through the bus (`credentials:get`). `@ax/credentials` talks to `@ax/storage-sqlite` only through `storage:set` / `storage:get`. Verified by `eslint.config.mjs` `no-restricted-imports`.
- **I3 (no half-wired plugins):** Both plugins are reachable from the acceptance canary (`mcp-client.e2e.test.ts`) — it invokes `credentials:set` to stash an API key, `@ax/mcp-client` resolves it via `credentials:get` at connect time.
- **I4 (one source of truth):** MCP server configs live exclusively in `@ax/storage-sqlite` via the `mcp:servers` index key + per-server config keys. `@ax/mcp-client` never caches configs outside what it needs for an active connection. Credentials live exclusively in `@ax/storage-sqlite` via the `credential:` prefix.
- **I5 (capabilities explicit and minimized):** Host-side plugins with no IPC exposure; stdio MCP subprocess env is an allowlist (not inherit-and-filter); inline secrets in config are rejected at parse time, not just warned. MCP subprocesses run host-side because granting the sandbox network egress + credential decryption would widen the trust boundary for no gain.

## CI

`pnpm build`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm lint` — all clean on `feat/week-6.5e-mcp-client`. Test count **580 / 580** green.

## Files of note

- `.changeset/week-6-5e-mcp-client.md` — per-package version bumps (`@ax/credentials` minor, `@ax/mcp-client` minor, `@ax/cli` patch, `@ax/test-harness` patch).
- `packages/credentials/SECURITY.md` — credentials threat-model review.
- `packages/mcp-client/SECURITY.md` — mcp-client threat-model review.
- `packages/cli/src/__tests__/mcp-client.e2e.test.ts` — acceptance canary.
- `packages/test-harness/src/mcp-server-stub.ts` — the stdio MCP stub used by the canary.

# Week 7–9 — Production Deployment Shape (PR notes)

**Branch:** `feat/week-7-9-k8s-deployment`
**Base:** `main` @ `0959a2d` (tip of 6.5e)
**Plan:** [`docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-7-9-k8s-deployment.md`](2026-04-24-week-7-9-k8s-deployment.md)
**Handoff:** [`docs/plans/2026-04-23-week-7-9-handoff.md`](2026-04-23-week-7-9-handoff.md)

---

## Summary

Week 7–9 swaps the single-host plugins shipped in earlier slices for their
production-shape equivalents behind the same hook surfaces. This is the slice
where Invariant 1 ("hook surface is transport-/storage-agnostic") earns its
keep — we ship a postgres + k8s + git-backed plugin set against subscriber
contracts that haven't moved an inch since Week 4–6, and a `MockWorkspace` ↔
`@ax/workspace-git` shared contract test suite proves the workspace abstraction
in Section 4.5 is genuinely backend-shape-neutral.

Eight new plugins, the workspace contract in `@ax/core`, the `@ax/preset-k8s`
meta-package, and a Helm chart ported from legacy. `pnpm test` is green (708
tests across 24 packages + 1 preset).

---

## Five-invariant audit

| Invariant | Status | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| **I1** — transport-/storage-agnostic hooks | ✅ | `packages/test-harness/src/workspace-contract.ts` runs identical 9-assertion suite against both `MockWorkspace` (versions like `mock-N`, no SHA shape) and `@ax/workspace-git` (versions are commit SHAs) — proves no git vocab leaks into payload field names. `socketPath → runnerEndpoint` rename (commit `16bb1f4`) cleans up the I1 violation 6.5a introduced before k8s was on the table. |
| **I2** — no cross-plugin imports | ✅ | All new plugins depend on `@ax/core` + third-party deps only. Storage-postgres reaches Kysely via `bus.call('database:get-instance')`; eventbus-postgres + session-postgres take their own `connectionString` because LISTEN can't share a pool (documented precedent). Confirmed via `grep -rn "from '@ax/" packages/{database-postgres,storage-postgres,eventbus-postgres,session-postgres,workspace-git,sandbox-k8s}/src/` — only `@ax/core`. |
| **I3** — no half-wired plugins | ✅ | Every new plugin is loaded by `@ax/preset-k8s` (`presets/k8s/src/index.ts`). Real exercise: postgres trio via testcontainers in their own suites; `@ax/preset-k8s` acceptance test (`presets/k8s/src/__tests__/acceptance.test.ts`) drives storage / eventbus / session / workspace through the bus end-to-end against a real `postgres:16-alpine` container. `@ax/preset-local` regression — `pnpm --filter @ax/cli test` 41/41 green after Phase B landed. |
| **I4** — one source of truth per concept | ✅ | `WorkspaceVersion` brand declared once in `@ax/core` (`packages/core/src/workspace.ts`), re-exported from `@ax/ipc-protocol` for compat. Postgres tables use per-plugin prefixes (`storage_postgres_v1_*`, `session_postgres_v1_*`); no cross-plugin foreign keys (defensive comment in `session-postgres/src/migrations.ts`). The wiring smoke test (`presets/k8s/src/__tests__/preset.test.ts`) catches duplicate service-hook registrants at compose time. |
| **I5** — capabilities explicit and minimized | ✅ | sandbox-k8s pod defaults are locked down: `runtimeClassName=gvisor`, `automountServiceAccountToken=false`, `runAsNonRoot=true`, `allowPrivilegeEscalation=false`, `capabilities.drop=['ALL']`, `readOnlyRootFilesystem=true` + emptyDir mounts, resource limits, `activeDeadlineSeconds=3600`. Host pod RBAC is the minimum five `pods` verbs (`pods: create/delete/get/list/watch`) — no `pods/exec`, no `pods/attach`, no cluster-scoped verbs. eventbus-postgres channel-name allowlist + `pg.escapeIdentifier` + `pg_notify($1,$2)` parameter binding + 8000-byte payload cap. workspace-git path validation rejects `..`/absolute/NUL/`.git/` segments via the legacy `safePath` helper ported into `@ax/core`. |

---

## Boundary review

- **Alternate impl for `workspace:apply` / `read` / `list` / `diff`:** `@ax/workspace-gcs` (manifest-object pattern, Section 4.5) and `@ax/workspace-s3` are the obvious candidates. The contract test-suite (`runWorkspaceContract`) is precisely the suite they will be required to pass.
- **Alternate impl for `database:get-instance`:** a future `@ax/database-mysql` or `@ax/database-cockroach` would register the same hook returning a Kysely instance for that dialect.
- **Alternate impl for `eventbus:emit` / `subscribe`:** `@ax/eventbus-inprocess` (this slice) and `@ax/eventbus-postgres` (this slice) are the two impls that validate the contract on day one. A future redis/nats impl would slot in identically.
- **Alternate impl for `sandbox:open-session`:** `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` (already shipped) and `@ax/sandbox-k8s` (this slice). Future firecracker impl would register the same hook.
- **Payload field names that might leak:** none. `parentVersion`/`version` are already in IPC protocol from 6.5a; we keep them. `runnerEndpoint` is now an opaque URI (was `socketPath`). `commitRef` survives in the wire schema as the runner's opaque local identifier — host doesn't dispatch on it; the `changes` array IS the source of truth.
- **Subscriber risk:** `workspace:applied` will be the integration point for `@ax/scanner-canary` (Week 10–12) and `@ax/skills-validator` (Week 13+). Lazy `contentBefore`/`contentAfter` fetchers (covered by contract test) ensure subscribers that only care about specific path globs don't pay for full diff bytes.
- **Wire surface:** `workspace.commit-notify` IPC schema gains `changes: FileChange[]` (default `[]`). Backwards-compatible with runners that don't yet send a diff — they get a no-op apply. Wire response NEVER carries a delta — lazy fetchers don't serialize, exposing them widens the trust boundary.

---

## Per-package security review

Three-line summaries — full details in each `SECURITY.md`.

### `@ax/workspace-git` (`packages/workspace-git/SECURITY.md`)
- **Sandbox:** filesystem reach limited to `repoRoot/repo.git`; path validation rejects `..`/absolute/NUL/`.git/` segments at `apply` boundary; reads/lists/diffs go through `git.readBlob`/`listFiles` (no direct FS path resolution from caller-supplied strings); zero shell-out (pure `isomorphic-git`).
- **Injection:** agent-supplied `reason` flows into commit messages only — opaque to consumers; LLM-output `FileChange.content` is `Bytes`, never interpolated; bot identity (`ax-runner`) hard-coded — agent-supplied identity goes to `WorkspaceDelta.author` provenance fields, never git committer.
- **Supply chain:** `isomorphic-git@1.37.5` (pinned, no install hooks beyond a publish-time `prepublishOnly` that's irrelevant for npm registry installs) + `picomatch@4.0.4` (pinned). The `simple-get` transitive is network-capable but unreachable from our `git/http/node` non-import.

### `@ax/database-postgres` (`packages/database-postgres/SECURITY.md`)
- **Sandbox:** opens a `pg.Pool` to caller-config'd `connectionString`; pool max bounded (default 10); password never logged at info+; no FS / spawn / network beyond the configured DSN.
- **Injection:** N/A — exposes a Kysely instance; consumers parametrize their own queries (Kysely does it by default).
- **Supply chain:** `kysely@0.28.16`, `pg@8.20.0` — both pinned exactly, established maintainers, no install hooks.

### `@ax/storage-postgres` (`packages/storage-postgres/SECURITY.md`)
- **Sandbox:** reads/writes one table (`storage_postgres_v1_kv`); Kysely parametrizes; migrations are pinned literal SQL.
- **Injection:** tool/LLM output may flow into `storage:set` value bytes; stored as opaque BYTEA; never interpolated elsewhere in this plugin.
- **Supply chain:** no new direct deps beyond database-postgres's review.

### `@ax/eventbus-postgres` (`packages/eventbus-postgres/SECURITY.md`)
- **Sandbox:** dedicated `pg.Client` per instance; channel-name allowlist `/^[a-zA-Z0-9_]+$/` + `pg.escapeIdentifier` on LISTEN/UNLISTEN; `pg_notify($1,$2)` parameter-bound on emit; 8000-byte payload cap; reconnect backoff bounded 1s→30s.
- **Injection:** subscriber payloads are caller-supplied; JSON-serialized in transit; LISTEN/NOTIFY observable by any postgres role with sufficient privileges (flagged).
- **Supply chain:** direct dep `pg@8.20.0` already covered by trio review.

### `@ax/session-postgres` — no SECURITY.md yet
Walked but not committed. Same threat profile as eventbus-postgres + storage-postgres combined. Known limit: the LISTEN client lacks reconnect-with-backoff that eventbus-postgres has; on a postgres restart, in-flight blocked claims fall through to their `timeoutMs` rather than waking. **Flagged as a follow-up in this PR description and as a code TODO.**

### `@ax/sandbox-k8s` (`packages/sandbox-k8s/SECURITY.md`)
- **Sandbox:** the single biggest blast-radius surface in the slice. Pod defaults: gvisor, runAsNonRoot=true, allowPrivilegeEscalation=false, capabilities.drop=['ALL'], readOnlyRootFilesystem=true with emptyDir for /tmp + /workspace, resource limits, activeDeadlineSeconds=3600 safety net. `automountServiceAccountToken=false` — runner pods get NO k8s API access. Host pod's k8s capability is set in the Helm chart's Role — this plugin's safety story depends on that RBAC scope being minimal (cross-reference to `deploy/charts/ax-next/SECURITY.md`).
- **Injection:** pod spec built from validated config + caller-supplied (Zod-validated) `sessionId`/`runnerBinary`; LLM output never reaches `buildPodSpec`; no `process.env[userInput]`.
- **Supply chain:** `@kubernetes/client-node@1.4.0` — pinned exactly, official k8s-sigs maintainer, no install hooks (only a publish-time `prepare`). Substantial bundle (~46MB unpacked) — trade-off is hand-rolling auth/CA-bundle/proxy support is worse.

### `deploy/charts/ax-next/SECURITY.md`
- **Sandbox:** Role lists every verb with rationale (`pods: create/delete/get/list/watch` only); legacy verbs explicitly dropped (`pods/exec`, `pods/attach`, `pods/log`, `patch`); two NetworkPolicies fence runner pods + host pod with documented ingress/egress rules.
- **Injection:** N/A — manifests are static templates with no model/tool input flowing in.
- **Supply chain:** `bitnami/postgresql@16.7.27` (chart digest committed in `Chart.lock`), `Bitnami's bitnamilegacy/* migration noted, CVE check honestly flagged as "not exhaustive — re-review."

---

## Acceptance evidence

### Automated (CI)

```
$ pnpm test
... (24 packages + 1 preset, all green)
Total tests: 708
```

Highlights of this slice's contribution:
- `@ax/core` workspace contract: 4 tests + 9 safe-path tests (68 total)
- `@ax/test-harness` MockWorkspace + shared contract: 25 tests
- `@ax/eventbus-inprocess`: 3 tests
- `@ax/database-postgres`: 3 tests (testcontainers)
- `@ax/storage-postgres`: 3 tests (testcontainers)
- `@ax/eventbus-postgres`: 6 tests (testcontainers, including cross-instance NOTIFY delivery)
- `@ax/session-postgres`: 13 tests (testcontainers, including cross-instance long-poll wakeup)
- `@ax/workspace-git`: 10 tests (1 scaffold + 9 contract)
- `@ax/sandbox-k8s`: 29 tests (mocked k8s API)
- `@ax/preset-k8s`: 10 tests (5 wiring + 5 acceptance with testcontainers postgres)

The `@ax/preset-k8s` acceptance test is the integration proof: bootstraps the
full plugin chain against a real postgres container, exercises storage /
eventbus / session / workspace through the bus, asserts state lands. ~2.3s
warm, ~25–40s cold (postgres image pull).

### Manual (kind / real cluster)

See [`deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md`](../../deploy/MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md). **Honest
disclosure:** the HTTP runner-IPC client is deferred (Task 14b cut). The kind
acceptance in this slice can verify chart installs cleanly + postgres + host
pod come up + runner pod is created — but the chat itself fails at the IPC
handshake until HTTP IPC ships. We mark the chat acceptance criterion as N/A
for this slice; re-enable once the follow-up unblocks it.

---

## Scope cuts deferred to follow-up PRs

Each is called out in either code (TODO comments), docs (SECURITY.md "Known
limits" sections, MANUAL-ACCEPTANCE.md "Known gotchas"), or both.

- **HTTP runner-IPC** — `@ax/agent-runner-core/src/ipc-client.ts` `http:` branch throws `HostUnavailableError("not implemented yet")`. The runner-side HTTP server (mirror of `@ax/ipc-server` over TCP) doesn't exist yet. Substantial follow-up — multi-file but bounded.
- **`Dockerfile.agent`** — bundling both runner binaries into a container image. Build concern, not a chart concern.
- **`@ax/workspace-git-http`** — multi-replica workspace via separate git server. Single-replica only this slice.
- **Web proxy** — egress proxy for runner pods. Week 10+.
- **Admin / OAuth / agents / company-admin** — Week 9.5 multi-tenant slice.
- **`@ax/preset-local` workspace integration** — wire `@ax/workspace-git` into the local preset for parity with k8s preset. Currently the local preset doesn't register a workspace plugin.
- **`@ax/session-postgres` LISTEN reconnect-with-backoff** — eventbus-postgres has it; session-postgres LISTEN client doesn't.
- **Kernel shutdown lifecycle** — when this lands, eventbus-postgres can drop its `shutdown()` test escape hatch and database-postgres / storage-sqlite can release pools cleanly.
- **`kubeconform`/`kubeval` in CI** — would catch chart drift early. Not run locally for this PR.
- **HPA / PDB / multi-replica host** — single-replica only this slice.
- **`@ax/session-postgres/SECURITY.md`** — walked but not yet committed. Add in follow-up.

# Week 9.5 — multi-tenant model PR notes

Closes the multi-tenant primitives (auth + agents + admin API) for Week 9.5 per `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-9.5-multi-tenant-handoff.md`.

## Summary

- New plugins: `@ax/http-server` (pulled forward from Week 10-12), `@ax/auth-oidc` (Google OIDC + dev-bootstrap + cookie sessions), `@ax/agents` (entity + ACL), `@ax/teams` (membership + ACL).
- Admin API surface: `/admin/agents`, `/admin/mcp-servers`, `/admin/teams`, `/admin/me`, `/admin/sign-out`. Auth API: `/auth/sign-in/:provider`, `/auth/callback/:provider`, `/auth/sign-out`, `/auth/dev-bootstrap`.
- Chat path now resolves an agent (with ACL gate) before opening a sandbox; the runner reads its frozen agent config via a new `session.get-config` IPC action; `tool:list` is filtered to the agent's `allowedTools` + `mcpConfigIds`.
- One-shot CLI: `ax-next admin bootstrap` mints the first admin against a freshly-booted preset.
- Two enforcement rails: `no-restricted-imports` blocks cross-plugin imports; a custom `local/no-bare-tenant-tables` rule blocks `selectFrom('agents_v1_*' | 'auth_v1_*' | 'teams_v1_*')` outside `store.ts` / `scope.ts` / `__tests__/`.

## Plan + invariants

- Implementation plan: `docs/plans/2026-04-25-week-9.5-multi-tenant.md`.
- Handoff: `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-9.5-multi-tenant-handoff.md`.

| # | Invariant | Evidence |
|---|-----------|----------|
| I1 | Hook surface transport-agnostic | `userId` / `agentId` / `agentConfig` / `team_id` / `role` are identity-/model-vocab; no `oauth_*`, `pg_*`, `cookie`, `bearer`, `pod_name` field names appear in any payload. See per-task boundary review notes below; `packages/agents/src/types.ts`, `packages/teams/src/types.ts`, `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts:213` (session.get-config). |
| I2 | No cross-plugin imports | `eslint.config.mjs` `no-restricted-imports` covers `@ax/auth-oidc` / `@ax/agents` / `@ax/teams` / `@ax/http-server`. Commit `1bfd98f`. |
| I3 | No half-wired plugins | Every plugin reaches the canary acceptance test in `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/multi-tenant-acceptance.test.ts` (11 scenarios: 1-8 + hardening A/B/C). Commit `e79ec4e`. |
| I4 | One source of truth | Users live in `@ax/auth-oidc` (`auth_v1_users`); agents live in `@ax/agents` (`agents_v1_agents`); teams in `@ax/teams` (`teams_v1_teams` + `teams_v1_memberships`); sessions in `@ax/session-postgres` (v2 side-table). Cross-plugin reads go through service hooks (e.g., `teams:is-member`). |
| I5 | Capabilities explicit / minimized | Every manifest lists exact `registers` / `calls`. `teams:is-member` is intentionally NOT in `@ax/agents`'s `manifest.calls` so agents degrade to deny-by-default when teams plugin is absent. See `packages/agents/src/plugin.ts`, commit `c1375f9`. |
| I6 | Authz at API layer | Every admin route gates on `auth:require-user` BEFORE handing to the service hook. Hiding a button is not access control — there is no UI in this slice; auth is done at the wire. `packages/agents/src/admin-routes.ts:188-194`, `packages/teams/src/admin-routes.ts`, `packages/mcp-client/src/admin-routes.ts`. |
| I7 | Tenant queries scoped | `scopedAgents()` (`packages/agents/src/scope.ts:29-51`) + `scopedTeams()` (`packages/teams/src/scope.ts:26-39`); enforced by `local/no-bare-tenant-tables` rule (`eslint-rules/no-bare-tenant-tables.js`). Audit table below lists every `selectFrom` on tenant tables. |
| I8 | Untrusted prompts visible | `system_prompt` is user-authored and flows untrusted to the LLM. Documented at the type definitions (`packages/session-inmemory/src/types.ts`, `packages/agents/src/types.ts`). UI display deferred to Week 10-12. |
| I9 | Tokens never leak through hook returns | `auth:require-user` returns `{ user: { id, isAdmin } }` only; cookie set via `Set-Cookie`. Bearer tokens never appear in `agents:resolve` / `chat:run` payloads. `packages/auth-oidc/src/store.ts`, IPC schemas in `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts`. |
| I10 | Session ↔ agent immutability | `session_postgres_v2` side-table has PK on `session_id`, INSERT-once, no `updated_at`. Switching agents = new session. Commit `6d0c42a`. |
| I11 | IdP discovery pinned | `Issuer.discover()` runs ONCE at init against the `issuer` from plugin config; no auto-discovery from request-time input. `packages/auth-oidc/src/oidc.ts:78-102`. |
| I12 | Bootstrap one-shot + idempotent | `ax-next admin bootstrap` POST `/auth/dev-bootstrap`; server-side `upsertBootstrapAdmin` returns `{ user, created: false }` on second call. `packages/auth-oidc/src/store.ts:207-230`, `packages/cli/src/commands/admin/bootstrap.ts`. |

## What landed

20 commits since `610f5c7`:

```
e79ec4e test(multi-tenant): acceptance + 3 hardening scenarios
582dc08 feat(preset-k8s): wire http-server + auth + teams; mount admin routes
ecd23ea feat(cli): ax-next admin bootstrap
5616b2a feat(agents): complete team-visibility ACL via teams:is-member
9ce1430 feat(teams): /admin/teams* endpoints
4b17d02 feat(teams): team + membership entity + service hooks
acd2761 feat(auth): /admin/me + /admin/sign-out
24c65f5 feat(mcp-client): admin MCP-server CRUD with owner scoping
2cff25b feat(agents): admin agent CRUD endpoints with ACL
1bfd98f chore(lint): forbid cross-plugin imports + bare tenant queries
5f40e7a feat(tool-dispatcher): per-agent tool & MCP scoping
a39bdfb feat(ipc): session.get-config action for the runner
f126af5 feat(chat-orchestrator): require agents:resolve before sandbox open
3f88fb7 feat(session): payload gains userId + agentId + agentConfig
6d0c42a feat(session-postgres): v2 schema for {session_id, user_id, agent_id, agent_config_json}
c1375f9 feat(agents): agent entity + agents:resolve ACL gate
0da2c82 feat(auth): OIDC + dev-bootstrap flows + cookie sessions
52b7fe5 chore(auth): scaffold @ax/auth + pinned OIDC deps
55f7925 feat(http-server): signed cookies + CSRF guard
a57b3f4 feat(http-server): add plugin with http:register-route hook
```

New packages: `@ax/http-server`, `@ax/auth-oidc`, `@ax/agents`, `@ax/teams`. Substantial extensions to `@ax/session-postgres`, `@ax/session-inmemory`, `@ax/chat-orchestrator`, `@ax/tool-dispatcher`, `@ax/mcp-client`, `@ax/ipc-server`, `@ax/ipc-protocol`, `@ax/cli`, `presets/k8s`.

## Boundary review (consolidated)

Per-task boundary notes — alternate impl, payload field names that might leak, subscriber risk, wire surface.

- **`@ax/http-server`** — alternate: future `@ax/http-server-fastify` registers the same `http:register-route` shape. Field names: `method` / `path` / `handler` / `params` are HTTP-vocab; HTTP IS the alternate-impl set. Raw `IncomingMessage`/`ServerResponse` are never exposed — only an opaque `{req, res}` adapter. Subscriber risk: `http:request` payload has only `method` / `path` / `headers` (already lowercased/stringified, no `socket`). Wire surface: NOT an IPC action — routes are host-internal. CSRF subscriber's only capability is reject-with-4xx; cannot mutate the route table or peer plugins.
- **`@ax/auth-oidc`** — alternates: `@ax/auth-saml` / `@ax/auth-local-password` register the same `auth:require-user` / `auth:get-user` / `auth:create-bootstrap-user`. Field names: `auth_provider` / `auth_subject_id` are generic across IdPs (no `oauth_*` / `oidc_*`); `session_id` is internal — never appears in hook return payloads. Subscriber risk: `auth:user-signed-in` payload is `{userId, provider}` only — no tokens, no email leak. Wire surface: NONE — auth hooks are host-internal; agents never call auth.
- **`@ax/agents`** — alternate: future `@ax/agents-git` (file-backed) would register the same `agents:resolve` / `list-for-user` / `create` / `update` / `delete`. Field names: `owner_id` / `owner_type` / `visibility` / `workspace_ref` — generic; no git/k8s vocab. `workspace_ref` is opaque to subscribers, never parsed. Subscriber risk: `agents:resolved` payload has only `{agentId, userId, visibility}`; no `system_prompt` / tool list leak. Wire surface: 5 admin routes via `http:register-route`; bodies bounded ≤ 64 KiB; auth gate via `auth:require-user`.
- **`@ax/teams`** — alternate: `@ax/teams-ldap` / `@ax/teams-saml-groups` would map IdP groups → memberships and register the same surface. Field names: `team_id` / `user_id` / `role` / `display_name` — generic; no IdP / SCIM vocabulary. Subscriber risk: no subscriber payloads emitted. Wire surface: 5 admin routes; schemas in `packages/teams/src/types.ts`.
- **`@ax/session-postgres` (v2 schema)** — alternate: `@ax/session-inmemory` carries the same fields in-memory. Field names: `user_id` / `agent_id` / `agent_config_json` are per-plugin SQL identifiers, not hook-payload field names. Subscriber risk: N/A — internal storage. Wire surface: N/A — migration-only.
- **session payload (`session:create` / `session:resolve-token` / `session:get-config`)** — alternates: any session backend (inmemory, postgres, future redis). Field names: `userId` / `agentId` / `agentConfig` are identity-/model-vocab; `agentConfig` fields (`systemPrompt`, `allowedTools`, `mcpConfigIds`, `model`) are model-vocab — they ARE the runner's boot config. Subscriber risk: `agentConfig.systemPrompt` is USER-AUTHORED (an admin pasted it into POST `/admin/agents`). Documented at the type definition; downstream subscribers must treat as untrusted; intended sole destination is the LLM prompt. Wire surface: `session:create` / `session:resolve-token` are in-process only; `session.get-config` is an IPC action with `.strict({})` empty body schema (host reads `ctx.sessionId`, runner cannot ask for someone else's config).
- **`@ax/chat-orchestrator`** — alternate: `agents:resolve` already has two impls in this repo (`@ax/agents` postgres + `@ax/cli/dev-agents-stub`); a future `@ax/agents-git` registers the same shape. Field names: `agentConfig` is model-vocab; `owner.userId` / `agentId` are identity; `workspaceRoot` was already there from Week 4-6. Subscriber risk: `agentConfig.systemPrompt` carries USER-AUTHORED content; documented at the type definition as untrusted, intended for LLM prompt only. Wire surface: `sandbox:open-session` is in-process only — agents never call it.
- **`@ax/tool-dispatcher` (per-agent filter)** — alternate: filter is keyed off generic config fields (`allowedTools`, `mcpConfigIds`); MCP namespacing is a string prefix agreed with mcp-client's `buildNamespacedName`. No vocabulary leak. Field names: `scope = {allowedTools, mcpConfigIds}`. Subscriber risk: N/A — `tool:list` is a service hook, not subscribed. Wire surface: dispatcher already has `tool:list`; no new wire surface. `session:get-config` reached via the bus (host-internal).
- **`@ax/ipc` (`session.get-config` IPC action)** — alternate: same IPC schema works against any session backend (postgres/inmemory). Field names: `agentConfig` fields are model-vocab — that's the whole point of the payload. Subscriber risk: `agentConfig.systemPrompt` is USER-AUTHORED; consumer-side comments in the runners flag this; the LLM is its sole legitimate destination. Wire surface: NEW IPC action; schema in `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts:213`; timeout 5s in `timeouts.ts`.
- **`@ax/mcp-client` (admin CRUD)** — alternate: future `@ax/mcp-client` variants (e.g., remote registry-backed) would mount the same routes. Field names: `ownerId` / `id` / `transport` — all generic. `credentialRefs` is the existing credential-indirection pattern from 6.5e. Subscriber risk: no new subscriber payloads. Wire surface: 6 admin routes; `/test` endpoint connects to the configured remote (the only outbound network reach).
- **`presets/k8s` admin wiring** — alternate: any preset can opt into admin routes by passing `mountAdminRoutes: true`. Field names: `team_id` / `user_id` / `role` — generic. Wire surface: 5 admin routes per plugin; bodies ≤ 64 KiB; auth + role gates at the API.

## Security review (consolidated)

### Sandbox

Net-new capabilities are minimal and bounded.

- One HTTP listener (host:port from preset config; bound by Node's socket-level timeouts).
- `/admin/*` routes accept JSON bodies bounded ≤ 64 KiB; `/auth/*` routes accept bodies ≤ 1 MiB (the `@ax/http-server` default).
- POST `/admin/mcp-servers/:id/test` opens an outbound MCP connection bounded by a 30-second timeout. Errors are sanitized — only `error.code` / `error.name` surfaces; no stack traces or remote response bodies bubble to the caller.
- `@ax/ipc-server` gains a `session.get-config` action authenticated by the runner's existing bearer token. The handler reads `ctx.sessionId` (set by the IPC server's auth gate after the bearer token resolves), not a body parameter; the schema is `.strict({})`. The runner cannot request another session's config.
- Rate-limit subscriber on `/auth/*` (30/min/IP) is reject-only — it cannot mutate the route table or peer-plugin state.

No FS / process-spawn / new-network reach beyond what existed pre-9.5. The `@ax/http-server` CSRF subscriber's only capability is to reject with 4xx; it cannot mutate `ctx`, the route table, or peer plugins.

### Injection

The new high-risk surfaces and how we treat them.

- **`system_prompt` is user-authored** and flows untrusted to the LLM in `chat:run` (Invariant I8). For team agents, a different user's prompt can run on this user's behalf. We brand the field as untrusted at every type definition and downstream subscriber comment, but the LLM is its sole legitimate destination. UI display before chat starts is the Week 10-12 follow-up.
- **Cookies are HMAC-SHA256-signed** with a 32-byte key from `AX_HTTP_COOKIE_KEY` (or `cookieKey` opt for tests). Tampered values return null without throwing. We use `crypto.timingSafeEqual` for the HMAC compare; HMAC-segment length-mismatch and base64url-charset mismatch short-circuit before `timingSafeEqual` so we never throw on caller-supplied input. The cookie value cap is 4 KiB.
- **CSRF guard** on POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE: exact-match Origin allow-list OR `X-Requested-With: ax-admin` header. State-changing requests with neither return 403 `csrf-failed:{origin-missing|origin-mismatch}`. The allow-list is exact-string match — no URL parsing, no wildcards.
- **IdP errors are NEVER logged verbatim** — only `error.code` surfaces (`auth_callback_failed` event). State mismatches return 400 `callback-failed` without echoing the cookie. `dev-bootstrap` token comparison uses `timingSafeEqual` gated on a length pre-check.
- **Bodies are zod-validated** on every admin endpoint. Fields are length-capped (`displayName` 1-128, `systemPrompt` ≤ 32 KiB, `team_id` / `user_id` length-capped). `model` is allow-listed via `AX_AGENT_MODELS_ALLOWED` (env, comma-separated, with a built-in default).
- **Wildcard tool scope** (`allowedTools=[]` AND `mcpConfigIds=[]`) is the dev-only sentinel for the dev-agents stub. Production `/admin/agents` REJECTS this combination at the API (`packages/agents/src/admin-routes.ts:273`).
- **Role checks happen via SQL filter** on `teams_v1_memberships` — never trusting an actor payload alone. Role is strict-equality `'admin' | 'member'`.
- **`session_id` is 32 random bytes** via `crypto.randomBytes`; comparisons go through `@ax/http-server`'s HMAC verifier (`timingSafeEqual`).

### Supply chain

Two new deps, both pinned EXACT (no caret/tilde):

- `openid-client@5.7.0`
- `jose@5.10.0`

Both maintained by [@panva](https://github.com/panva) (Filip Skokan, OIDC reference implementer). Both MIT.

We verified via `npm view`: `jose@5.10.0` has no scripts; `openid-client@5.7.0` has only dev scripts (`test`, `format`) — no `postinstall` / `preinstall` / `prepare`.

Transitive surface (`pnpm -r why`):
- `openid-client@5.7.0` → `jose@^4.15.9` (pinned to 4.15.9 transitively), `lru-cache@^6.0.0`, `object-hash@^2.2.0`, `oidc-token-hash@^5.0.3`.
- `jose@5.10.0` → no dependencies.

`openid-client@6.8.3` was already in the lockfile via `@kubernetes/client-node`; our 5.x pin is isolated to `@ax/auth-oidc`.

`zod` is already a workspace dep (used by `mcp-client`); admin endpoints reuse it for body validation.

## Cross-tenant query audit

**Result:** all 49 catalogued queries fall into one of: scope helper, store.ts under ACL pre-check at the call site, or `__tests__/`. The `local/no-bare-tenant-tables` rule prevents anyone from adding a bare query outside these locations without tripping CI.

## Cookie + CSRF posture

- **HttpOnly:** yes, always. Set unconditionally in `buildSetCookieHeader` (`packages/http-server/src/cookies.ts:154`).
- **SameSite:** `Lax` by default; if a caller explicitly passes `None`, `Secure` is forced (per spec).
- **Secure:** derived from `req.protocol` or `X-Forwarded-Proto` when `AX_TRUST_PROXY=1`; falls back to `false` for HTTP-localhost dev.
- **Signing:** HMAC-SHA256 with a 32-byte key from `AX_HTTP_COOKIE_KEY` (64 hex or 44 base64 chars). Wire format `<base64url(value)>.<base64url(hmac)>`.
- **CSRF:** on POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE — exact-match Origin allow-list OR `X-Requested-With: ax-admin`. State-changing requests with neither return 403 `csrf-failed:{origin-missing|origin-mismatch}`. Empty Origin allow-list emits a stderr warn unless `AX_HTTP_ALLOW_NO_ORIGINS=1`.
- **Cookie value cap:** 4 KiB (storage-exhaustion guard).
- **Tamper handling:** length-mismatch and base64url-charset mismatch short-circuit before `timingSafeEqual`; tampered values return null without throwing.

## IdP allow-list

- Google OIDC configured via `AX_AUTH_GOOGLE_*` env vars in the preset (issuer, client_id, client_secret, redirect_uri).
- **Issuer URL is pinned at config time.** No auto-discovery from caller-supplied issuer. `Issuer.discover()` runs ONCE at init against `args.config.issuer` (`packages/auth-oidc/src/oidc.ts:82`).
- **Redirect URI is exact-matched.** The IdP and our config must agree byte-for-byte; `openid-client` validates this against the discovered metadata and the callback parameters.
- **Dev-bootstrap path:** refused at init when `NODE_ENV === 'production'` AND requires a non-empty `AX_DEV_BOOTSTRAP_TOKEN`. Token comparison uses `timingSafeEqual` gated on length pre-check.

## Rate-limit posture

- `/auth/sign-in/*`, `/auth/callback/*`, `/auth/dev-bootstrap`: 30/min/IP via in-memory token bucket.
- IP source: first value of `x-forwarded-for` (lowercased + trimmed) when `AX_TRUST_PROXY=1`; otherwise a fixed `'local'` bucket (degraded; documented).
- **Multi-replica limitation:** the bucket lives in process memory, so each replica has its own. Plan defers the multi-replica fix to Week 13+ via `@ax/eventbus-postgres`. For MVP this is acceptable; the abuse mitigation it provides (slowing automated dev-bootstrap brute-force) still works against single-target replicas.

## Outstanding follow-ups (out of scope but tracked)

- **Important issue from Task 1 code review:** `/admin/*` routes log to stderr via `process.stderr.write` rather than `ctx.logger`. Repo-wide pattern that `ipc-http` also has; raise as a separate PR.
- **Important issue from Task 1:** the aborted-request observer doesn't fire `http:response-sent` for client-aborted requests. Fix in the next `http-server` iteration.
- **Wildcard sentinel review:** Task 7 chose `{allowedTools:[], mcpConfigIds:[]}` = pass-through (matches `dev-agents-stub` contract). Production agents are protected by Task 9's API rejection. Audit confirms; defer further hardening to Week 13+ (move sentinel to an explicit `'*'` literal so the empty-arrays case can be a proper deny-by-default).
- **Task 14:** GET `/admin/agents` doesn't yet thread `teamIds` for team-agent visibility. Chat path works (Task 17 scenario 6); list path returns personal-only. Open ticket for follow-up; one-line patch in `agents:list-for-user` callsite.
- **Task 17:** MCP scoping verified via fixture tools, not full POST `/admin/mcp-servers` → reload (the `mcp-client` loads configs at init only). Dynamic MCP reload is post-MVP.
- **Task 12:** the cannot-remove-last-admin guard is count-then-delete (TOCTOU). Acceptable for MVP; a serializable transaction is the proper fix.

## Acceptance test list

- `presets/k8s/src/__tests__/multi-tenant-acceptance.test.ts` — 11 scenarios covering acceptance 1-8 + hardening A/B/C.
  - 1: dev-bootstrap mints initial admin; admin endpoints accept the cookie.
  - 2: two distinct users authenticate.
  - 3: User A creates personal agent; chat:run completes.
  - 4: User B's GET `/admin/agents` returns User B's only.
  - 5: User B chat:run on User A's agentId → `terminated/agent-resolve:forbidden`, sandbox NOT opened.
  - 6: team agent path — User A creates team, adds User B, creates team-agent; User B chat:run completes.
  - 7: per-agent MCP scoping — User A's session sees `mcp.fs.*` tools; User B's doesn't.
  - 8: anonymous request → 401.
  - A: CSRF — POST `/admin/agents` from foreign Origin without `X-Requested-With` → 403.
  - B: cookie tamper — flipping one base64url char in HMAC segment → 401.
  - C: token reuse after sign-out — cookie no longer authenticates.
- Per-package tests across the slice: `@ax/http-server`, `@ax/auth-oidc`, `@ax/agents`, `@ax/teams`, `@ax/mcp-client`, `@ax/tool-dispatcher`, `@ax/chat-orchestrator`, `@ax/session-postgres`, `@ax/session-inmemory`, `@ax/ipc-server`, `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`, `@ax/cli`, `presets/k8s`, eslint-rules.

## What's deferred (re-statement from handoff)

- Role-based access within a team (admin vs member) → Week 13+ if teams outgrow flat membership.
- Public agents (anyone-signed-in access) → Week 13+.
- SAML, LDAP → Week 13+.
- SSO session revocation beyond cookie expiration → Week 13+.
- Audit log of admin actions as a dedicated audit trail → Week 10–12's `@ax/audit` absorbs this.
- Fine-grained workspace ACLs (per-file, per-path) → not in roadmap.
- Agent definitions as code/config (git-backed) → Week 13+.
- Hot-reload of plugin config (manifest invariant: config at construction).
- Multi-replica rate-limit coordination (in-memory token bucket only; promote to eventbus-based in Week 13+).

## Test plan (for the PR)

- [ ] `pnpm test` clean (~1200+ tests; postgres/Docker required for some).
- [ ] `pnpm build` clean.
- [ ] `pnpm lint` clean (no new errors; pre-existing 3 in `@ax/auth-oidc` tests).
- [ ] Manual: `ax-next admin bootstrap` against a freshly-booted preset; create agent; `curl /admin/me`.
